title: Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications
subtitle: The estimated death rate from [neonatal](#dod:neonatal) [preterm birth complications](#dod:preterm-birth-complications) in children under five years old, per 100,000.
caption:
Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications, 2023

The estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000.

This choropleth map displays the estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old per 100,000 population for the year 2023. The visualization utilizes a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow for the lowest rates to deep navy blue for the highest rates, with specific thresholds marked at 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, and 1,000 deaths per 100,000. The geographic distribution shows a distinct concentration of the highest mortality rates across sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia. Countries such as Somalia, Chad, and Nigeria appear in the darkest blue shades, placing them in the highest bands of the scale, likely exceeding 200 deaths per 100,000. India and Pakistan also display deep blue coloring, indicating similarly elevated rates within the upper tiers of the legend. In contrast, the lowest rates are visible across most of Western Europe, including France, Germany, and the United Kingdom, as well as in Australia, Canada, and the United States, where the map shows pale yellow or very light green hues corresponding to the 0 to 10 range. Russia and China fall into the light green category, suggesting rates between 10 and 20 per 100,000. Several regions, particularly in the central Pacific and parts of the Arctic, are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate that no data is available for those areas. The data presents a clear global disparity where the burden of death from neonatal preterm birth complications is heavily concentrated in specific developing regions while remaining minimal in high-income nations.
